description: HEXACO-60 personality inventory items. 10 items per dimension, 6 dimensions = 60 items total. reverse=true means the raw score (1-5) must be reversed: reversed = 6 - raw.

Honesty-Humility.description: Measures sincerity, fairness, greed-avoidance, and modesty. High scorers are sincere, honest, faithful, and uninterested in social status or material gain.

items.text: If I want something from a person I dislike, I will act very nicely toward that person in order to get it.
Having a lot of money is not especially important to me.
I wouldn't use flattery to get a raise or promotion at work, even if I thought it would be effective.
If I could get anything I wanted by deceiving others, I would do so.
I would never accept a bribe, even if it were very large.
I am an ordinary person who is no better than others.
I would be tempted to use counterfeit money, if I were confident I wouldn't get caught.
I think that I am entitled to more respect than the average person is.
I would like to live in a very expensive, high-class neighborhood.
I would never take things that don't belong to me, even if I were sure I would never get caught.

Emotionality.description: Measures fearfulness, anxiety, dependence, and sentimentality. High scorers feel strong emotional bonds, fear physical danger, and need emotional support.

items.text: I feel strong emotions when someone close to me is leaving for a long period of time.
I worry a lot less than most people do.
Even in an emergency I wouldn't feel like panicking.
I need the help of other people when I am having personal problems.
I feel very anxious in situations where there is some danger involved.
When I suffer from a painful experience, I need someone to make me feel comfortable.
I remain calm in situations that would make most other people extremely tense.
I feel very moved when I see a homeless person.
I rarely express my emotions openly.
I sometimes can't help worrying about little things.

Extraversion.description: Measures social self-esteem, social boldness, sociability, and liveliness. High scorers feel positively about themselves, enjoy social situations, and feel lively and energetic.

items.text: People see me as somewhat cold and distant.
People think of me as someone who has a lot of fun.
On most days, I feel cheerful and optimistic.
I feel comfortable around people.
In social situations, I'm usually the one who makes the first move.
I rarely find it difficult to be upbeat and optimistic.
I don't talk a lot.
I am a cheerful, lively person.
I seldom feel lonely or sad.
I feel that I am an unpopular person.

Agreeableness.description: Measures forgivingness, gentleness, flexibility, and patience. High scorers are willing to compromise, have little temper, and are not judgmental.

items.text: I tend to be lenient in judging other people.
Most people tend to get angry more quickly than I do.
People sometimes tell me that I am too critical of others.
I am usually quite flexible in my opinions when people disagree with me.
I feel that most people are basically well-intentioned.
I find it hard to fully forgive others who have done something terrible to a friend of mine.
If someone treated me badly, I would try to treat that person in just as bad a way.
I am a fairly patient person.
When someone does something bad to me, I try to forgive that person quickly.
People sometimes tell me that I'm too stubborn.

Conscientiousness.description: Measures organization, diligence, perfectionism, and prudence. High scorers are well-organized, hard-working, detail-oriented, and careful about decisions.

items.text: I make decisions based on the feeling of the moment rather than on careful deliberation.
I always try to be accurate in my work, even at the risk of taking more time.
When working on something, I don't pay much attention to small details.
I often push myself very hard when trying to achieve a goal.
I plan ahead and organize my activities.
I make a lot of mistakes because I don't think before I act.
I am an extremely productive person.
I always keep my promises, even if it would be convenient for me to break them.
I often spend so much time on one thing that I neglect other important things I need to do.
I like to have all my possessions organized and in order.

Openness to Experience.description: Measures aesthetic appreciation, inquisitiveness, creativity, and unconventionality. High scorers are imaginative, curious about art and science, and open to new ideas.

items.text: I think that paying attention to radical ideas is a waste of time.
I enjoy looking at maps of places I have never been.
I would enjoy creating a work of art, such as a novel, a song, or a painting.
I think of myself as someone who is interested in art, music, and literature.
I don't think of myself as the artistic or creative type.
I have a wild imagination.
I find it boring to discuss philosophy.
I am interested in learning about the history and politics of other countries.
I would find the life of an academic researcher appealing.
I find it difficult to keep my mind focused on something for a long period of time.
